provenance.source_corpus: Growing season carbon dioxide and methane exchange at a restored peatland on the Western Boreal Plain

source_spans.text: Restoration resulted in a shift in mean growing season fluxes from 378 g CO2 C and -0.2 g CH4 C at the unrestored site to -30 g CO2 C and 3.7 g CH4 C at the restored site
Restoration would decrease the CO2 source from peat fields
Restoration also resulted in a shift from a small CH4 sink to a CH4 source of 3.7 g CH4 C m-2 over the growing season
Water table position was significantly positively related to graminoid cover
Carbon dioxide exchange was correlated to vascular vegetation cover
Graminoid cover was also significantly related to log (GEPmax)
While on average restoration reduced GWP from CO2 and CH4 at both dry and wet restored plots
